buggy_code:
import pandas as pd

prices = pd.read_csv('prices.csv', index_col='date', parse_dates=True)

# Check missing data before filling
missing = prices.isna().sum()
print(f'Missing values before fill:\n{missing}')

# Forward fill with limit of 1 day only
prices_filled = prices.ffill(limit=1)

# Log remaining gaps after limited fill
still_missing = prices_filled.isna().sum()
if still_missing.any():
    print(f'Extended gaps remaining (not filled):\n{still_missing[still_missing > 0]}')

# Drop remaining NaN rather than filling extended gaps
prices_clean = prices_filled.dropna()

print(f'Rows before: {len(prices)}, after: {len(prices_clean)}')
clean_code:
import pandas as pd

prices = pd.read_csv('prices.csv', index_col='date', parse_dates=True)

missing = prices.isna().sum()
print(f'Missing values before fill:\n{missing}')

prices_filled = prices.ffill(limit=1)

still_missing = prices_filled.isna().sum()
if still_missing.any():
    print(f'Extended gaps remaining (not filled):\n{still_missing[still_missing > 0]}')

prices_clean = prices_filled.dropna()

print(f'Rows before: {len(prices)}, after: {len(prices_clean)}')
description: Clean example — forward fill with limit=1 and validation before and after.
explanation: Missing values are logged before filling. ffill(limit=1) only fills single-day gaps. Remaining NaN logged. Extended gaps dropped rather than silently filled.
